channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Judge rules head of watchdog agency must keep his job, says Trump's bid to oust him was unlawful
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The head of a federal watchdog agency must remain in his job, a judge in Washington ruled on Saturday, saying President Donald Trump's bid to remove the special counsel was unlawful. U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lifelong New York civil rights advocate and NAACP leader Hazel Dukes dies at 92
item.description: Hazel Dukes, the president of the New York State chapter of the NAACP and lifelong civil rights advocate, has died.
item.dc:creator: Sejal Govindarao, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump signs order designating English as the official language of the US
item.description: President Donald Trump signed on Saturday an executive order designating English as the official language of the United States.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Attorneys are suing to keep 10 migrants out of Guantanamo Bay as others say they were abused there
item.description: Civil rights attorneys are suing the Trump administration to prevent it from transferring 10 migrants detained in the U.S. to Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.
item.dc:creator: John Hanna, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo launches NYC mayoral bid
item.description: Former New York Gov. Andrew Cuomo launched his bid for New York City mayor with a video posted on his X account. This comes after much speculation that the governor would launch a comeback after leaving office in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump makes U.S. copper mining a focus of his domestic minerals policy
item.description: It could mean a new day for U.S. copper mining, and new worries for environmental groups that are contesting proposals such as the stalled Twin Metals project near northern Minnesota's Boundary Waters, a lake-filled wilderness on the U.S.-Canada border.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Ukraine loses 220 troops on same day of Trump and Zelenskyy spat
item.description: Ukraine lost 220 troops on the battlefield on the same day its president had a fiery fallout with President Donald Trump in the White House leading to the collapse of a mineral deal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Frustrations mount as Stefanik stuck in limbo
item.description: Frustrations are growing as Rep. Elise Stefanik’s (R-N.Y.) wait to be confirmed as ambassador to the United Nations could stretch until April due to the House’s ultra-slim majority. Stefanik is facing a unique problem. Despite numerous international crises facing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump and Zelenskyy through the years: From a 'perfect' call to an Oval Office meltdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The first time he spoke to Volodymyr Zelenskyy, President Donald Trump tried to pressure Ukraine's new leader to dig up dirt on Joe Biden ahead of the 2020 election. It was a 2019 phone call that eventually sparked an impeachment.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy leaves White House without signing minerals deal after Oval Office blowup
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump berated Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for being “disrespectful” Friday in an extraordinary Oval Office meeting, then abruptly called off the signing of a minerals deal that Trump said would have moved Ukraine closer to ending its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, ZEKE MILLER and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: JD Vance gets his long-awaited moment to admonish Ukraine's Zelenskyy
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Vice President JD Vance was dismissing Ukraine long before he upended an Oval Office meeting Friday by calling Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy “disrespectful” and asking if he had ever thanked the U.S. for its support. When Vance was a candidate for U.S.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What US lawmakers are saying about the White House clash between Trump and Zelenskyy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Key Republicans and Democrats in Congress have been stalwart supporters of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, but the blowup between the Ukrainian leader and President Donald Trump is threatening to change that.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What they said: Trump, Zelenskyy and Vance's heated argument in the Oval Office
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance on Friday berated Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy over the war in Ukraine, accusing him of not showing gratitude after he challenged Vance on the question of diplomacy with Russia's Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How the Trump, Zelensky meeting went off the rails
item.description: Efforts to negotiate an end to the war in Ukraine were left in tatters following President Trump’s confrontational White House meeting with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, which erupted into one of the most remarkable Oval Office scenes in years.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Politics Friday: Newly elected lawmakers reflect on their first legislative session
item.description: MPR News host Brian Bakst talks with two state representatives serving in their first legislative session. Later, a conversation with State Auditor Julie Blaha.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: US wants to end Ukraine war with little involvement: Ex-ambassador
item.description: President Donald Trump has met with European leaders during the week to discuss ending the war quickly.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Pro-Palestinian protesters occupy Barnard College, disrupting classes
item.description: According to Columbia University Apartheid Divest, the protesters demanded "the immediate reversal" of two student expulsions.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Rogers, NewsNation

item.title: NIH partially lifts freeze on funding process for medical research
item.description: Thousands of grant applications had been stalled when the Trump administration blocked the National Institutes of Health from posting notices to the Federal Register.
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein, MPR News

item.title: Out to Lunch: Speaker Demuth doesn’t want to be defined by one part of her identity
item.description: Earlier this month, Republican Rep. Lisa Demuth became the first Black person and female Republican to hold the role in the Minnesota House of Representatives. Minnesota Now sat down with her in a new series called, “Out to Lunch.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Adams asks to have charges dropped after leaked letter
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams’ lawyers filed a motion to have his charges dropped after someone “leaked” a letter from now-resigned U.S. Attorney Danielle Sasson, court records show.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Rep. Byron Donalds, backed by Trump, says he's running for Florida governor
item.description: Republican U.S. Rep. Byron Donalds announced Tuesday that he's entering the 2026 race to succeed Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis, who is ineligible to seek another term after eight years in office.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cicero Primary Election: Incumbent Dominick defeats Rodriguez, will serve 6th straight term
item.description: Incumbent Cicero Town President Larry Dominick will serve a fifth straight four-year term, defeating Esteban Rodriguez in the primary election Tuesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Dana Rebik, WGN 9

item.title: Candidates for NYC mayor work to get on the ballot as Cuomo looms large
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Tuesday was the first day when New York City mayoral candidates could collect signatures to gain access to the primary ballot in June. Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo continues to be favored in the race for mayor, despite not officially declaring.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Illegal marijuana taking a big bite out of Nevada tax revenue, school funding
item.description: The illicit market for cannabis in Nevada has grown to a $242 million business, taking a bite out of the state's effort to profit from the legalization of marijuana.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Zelensky expected to visit White House amid push for minerals deal
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky is expected to visit the White House on Friday as Kyiv and the Trump administration work to finalize an agreement on critical minerals.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Live updates: House Republicans adopts budget after Trump whips GOP holdouts
item.description: House Republican leadership is working to pass a budget resolution on Tuesday to pave the way for President Trump's agenda, but opposition has emerged from all sides of the conference.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5

item.title: No savings expected from nearly 40% of DOGE-canceled contracts
item.description: Nearly 40% of the federal contracts that the Trump administration claims to have canceled as part of its signature cost-cutting program aren’t expected to save the government any money, the administration's own data shows.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Watch: Trump, Macron hold White House press conference
item.description: President Donald Trump hosts French President Emmanuel Macron on Monday, the first visit by a European leader to the White House since Trump returned.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: Trump defends Musk’s ‘genius’ email to federal workers
item.description: President Trump on Monday defended tech billionaire Elon Musk after pushback over his email demanding federal workers list five accomplishments from the past week and in doing so, raising the of risk losing their jobs. “There was a lot of genius in sending it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump announces Dan Bongino as Deputy FBI Director
item.description: Bongino is a former U.S. Secret Service agent that became known for his defense of Trump while previously appearing as a Fox News contributor.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5
